title: lewisia rediviva var. minor
intro: Lewisia rediviva var. minor, also known as the bitterroot, is a perennial plant species native to the mountainous regions of western North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its bitter compounds, which have been found to possess antimicrobial and antifungal properties. The medicinal properties of Lewisia rediviva var. minor are attributed to its ability to stimulate digestion, relieve nausea, and treat various gastrointestinal issues. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and are said to offer health benefits such as improving digestion, reducing inflammation, and supporting immune function.
